title: Wren's Big Helper Day
synopsis: When Wren's little brother gets sick, she tries to help Mama by doing chores around the house. But her helpful attempts create silly messes that bring the whole family together for laughter and love.

pages.text: Wren woke up early. The sun was shining through her purple curtains. She could hear Mama talking to someone downstairs. "Time to get up!" Wren said to herself. She jumped out of bed and ran to find Mama.

illustration.description: A young Black girl with curly hair in purple pajamas stretching and yawning in a sunny bedroom. Purple curtains frame a bright window. Her room has colorful toys and books scattered around. The morning light creates a warm, golden glow.
illustration.alt: Wren waking up in her bedroom on a sunny morning

pages.text: In the kitchen, Mama was on the phone. She looked worried. "Yes, I understand," Mama said. "Thank you, Dr. Chen." Wren's little brother Ezra was sitting at the table. His nose was red and stuffy. "Achoo!" he sneezed.

illustration.description: A warm kitchen scene with Mama, a tall woman with natural hair, talking on the phone looking concerned. Little Ezra, a small boy with curly hair, sits at a wooden table with tissues nearby, looking sleepy and sniffly. Bright morning light streams through kitchen windows.
illustration.alt: Mama on phone while sick Ezra sits at kitchen table

pages.text: "Ezra has a cold," Mama explained to Wren. "He needs to rest today. I have to call work and stay home." Mama sighed. "There's so much to do around here." Wren's eyes lit up. "I can help!" she said. "I'm a big girl now!"

illustration.description: Wren standing proudly with her hands on her hips, chest puffed out with determination. Her curly hair bounces as she speaks. Mama smiles gently but looks tired. Ezra slumps sleepily in his chair with a small blanket around his shoulders.
illustration.alt: Wren volunteering to help while Mama looks tired and Ezra rests

pages.text: "I'll do the dishes!" Wren announced. She dragged a chair to the sink. The water was warm and bubbly. Wren scrubbed a plate. Splash! Water went everywhere. "Oops," she giggled. Soon the floor was covered in soapy puddles.

illustration.description: Wren standing on a wooden chair at a kitchen sink, soap bubbles floating everywhere. Water splashes onto the colorful tile floor creating puddles. Her pajamas are getting wet but she's smiling happily. Soap suds cover her hands and arms.
illustration.alt: Wren washing dishes with water splashing everywhere

pages.text: Next, Wren decided to make breakfast. She climbed on the counter to reach the cereal. The box tipped over. Crunchy circles scattered across the kitchen floor. "Breakfast is ready!" she called out. But nobody came running.

illustration.description: Wren on the kitchen counter reaching for a cereal box that's spilling colorful cereal pieces all over the floor. Her expression shows surprise and slight worry. The cereal creates a rainbow pattern on the wet, soapy floor from her earlier dish-washing adventure.
illustration.alt: Wren accidentally spilling cereal all over the kitchen floor

pages.text: Mama appeared in the doorway. Her eyes went wide. "Oh my!" she said. Wren's face turned red. "I wanted to help," she whispered. "I made a big mess instead." Tears started to form in her big brown eyes.

illustration.description: Mama standing in the kitchen doorway with a surprised but gentle expression, wearing a cozy robe. Wren looks small and sad in the middle of the messy kitchen with wet floors and scattered cereal. The scene shows empathy and understanding despite the chaos.
illustration.alt: Mama discovering the messy kitchen while Wren looks upset

pages.text: "Don't cry, sweetheart," Mama said with a hug. "You were trying to help. Let's clean this up together." They swept up the cereal and mopped the floor. "Can I try something else?" Wren asked. "Maybe the laundry?" Mama nodded carefully.

illustration.description: Mama hugging Wren tenderly in the kitchen. They're both holding cleaning supplies - Wren has a small dustpan and Mama has a mop. The scene shows warmth and forgiveness, with soft lighting making the moment feel cozy and loving.
illustration.alt: Mama hugging Wren while they clean up the kitchen together

pages.text: In the laundry room, Wren put clothes in the washing machine. She poured in the soap. Then she poured in more soap. "More soap means cleaner clothes!" she thought. She pressed the start button and smiled proudly.

illustration.description: Wren in a bright laundry room pouring way too much laundry detergent into the washing machine. Soap bubbles are already starting to form. She looks determined and happy. The laundry room has cheerful yellow walls and a window showing the backyard.
illustration.alt: Wren adding too much soap to the washing machine

pages.text: Twenty minutes later, soap bubbles crept under the laundry room door. They floated down the hallway like puffy white clouds. "Uh oh," said Wren. The bubbles kept coming and coming. Soon the whole hallway looked like a bubble party!

illustration.description: A hallway filled with enormous soap bubbles floating everywhere like a magical bubble wonderland. Wren stands in the middle looking amazed and slightly worried. The bubbles catch rainbow colors from the light, creating a whimsical but chaotic scene.
illustration.alt: Hallway filled with soap bubbles from the washing machine

pages.text: "What's happening?" called Ezra from the living room. Even with his stuffy nose, he could see the bubbles floating by. Mama came running. When she saw the bubble hallway, she started to laugh. "Oh, Wren!" she giggled.

illustration.description: Ezra peeking around the corner from the living room, wrapped in a colorful blanket with wide, amazed eyes looking at the floating bubbles. His expression shows wonder despite being sick. Bubbles drift around him like magic snowflakes.
illustration.alt: Sick Ezra amazed by the floating soap bubbles

pages.text: "I'm sorry, Mama," Wren said sadly. "I wanted to help, but I made everything worse." Mama knelt down and took Wren's hands. "You have a big heart," she said. "That's what matters most. But next time, let's work together from the start."

illustration.description: Mama kneeling down to Wren's level in the bubble-filled hallway, holding her daughter's small hands. Both have gentle, loving expressions. The bubbles create a magical backdrop around them, and the scene glows with warmth and understanding.
illustration.alt: Mama comforting Wren in the hallway full of bubbles

pages.text: Even Ezra wanted to help clean up. "I can pop bubbles!" he said with a stuffy giggle. The three of them worked together. They popped bubbles, cleaned the washing machine, and fixed the mess. It was actually fun working as a team.

illustration.description: All three family members happily popping soap bubbles together in the hallway. Ezra still has his blanket but is smiling and reaching for bubbles. Wren and Mama are laughing as they clean. The scene shows joy, teamwork, and family love.
illustration.alt: The whole family working together to clean up the soap bubbles

pages.text: After lunch, Ezra felt better and took a nap. Mama showed Wren how to help properly. They folded tiny washcloths together. They sorted socks by color. They watered the plants on the windowsill. Wren loved learning new ways to help.

illustration.description: Wren and Mama sitting together at a sunny table, folding small colorful washcloths. Neat piles of sorted socks sit nearby in rainbow colors. Through the window, green plants on the sill look happy and bright. The scene is peaceful and educational.
illustration.alt: Wren learning to help properly by folding laundry with Mama

pages.text: When Ezra woke up, he felt much better. "Thank you for taking care of me," he said to Wren. "You're the best big sister ever!" Wren beamed with pride. She had learned that helping means working together, not doing everything alone.

illustration.description: Ezra looking much healthier, hugging Wren while she smiles brightly. They're in the living room with soft afternoon light. Mama watches from the doorway with a proud, loving expression. The scene radiates family warmth and sibling love.
illustration.alt: Ezra thanking Wren for being a good big sister

pages.text: That evening, the family cuddled on the couch. "Today was crazy," Mama said with a smile. "But it was also wonderful." Wren snuggled closer. "Can we have another helper day tomorrow?" she asked. "With less bubbles!" Everyone laughed together.

illustration.description: The cozy family of three snuggled together on a comfortable couch in warm evening light. They're all smiling and relaxed, with soft blankets around them. The living room glows with table lamps, creating a perfect ending to their adventurous day.
illustration.alt: Family cuddling together on the couch at the end of the day

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Why did Ezra need to stay home?
choices: He didn't want to go to school
questions.explanation: Ezra had a cold with a stuffy nose, so Mama decided he needed to rest at home.

questions.question: What happened when Wren washed the dishes?
questions.explanation: When Wren tried to wash dishes, water splashed everywhere and made soapy puddles on the floor.

questions.question: Why did bubbles fill the hallway?
choices: Wren used too much laundry soap
questions.explanation: Wren put too much soap in the washing machine, which created lots of bubbles that floated into the hallway.

questions.question: How did the family clean up the bubbles?
questions.explanation: All three family members worked together to pop bubbles and clean up the mess, making it fun teamwork.

questions.question: What did Wren learn about helping?
choices: She should never try to help
She should only help when asked
questions.explanation: Wren learned that the best way to help is by working together with family, not trying to do everything alone.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Chapter 1
pages.instruction: Choose the best word to complete each sentence from Wren's story.

sentences.text: Wren woke up _____ and jumped out of bed.
Ezra was _____ and needed to rest.
Wren wanted to _____ Mama with the chores.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Chapter 3
pages.instruction: Complete these sentences about Wren's laundry adventure.

sentences.text: Wren put too much _____ in the washing machine.
The _____ floated down the hallway like clouds.
The family worked _____ to clean up the mess.
